snapshot.source_text: Replacing oxygen with a less electronegative nitrogen (74) leads to an expected blue-shift in the emission wavelength, from 562\mathrm{nm} to 528\mathrm{nm} .[70] However, substituting with a less electronegative carbon atom (76) surprisingly causes a red-shift in the emission. This is because nitrogen and oxygen have lone pairs that can donate into the pi-system, reducing their ability to stabilize the LUMO compared to carbon, despite being more electronegative. Interestingly, substituting carbon with silicon results in an even further red-shift for silicon-rhodamine 77, with an emission at 648\mathrm{nm} , despite silicon's lower electronegativity.[71] This is because the energy levels of atom's p-orbitals (Figure 9b) must be considered, since the FMOs are derived from the linear combination of atomic orbitals (LCAOs).[79] Silicon possesses 3p orbitals, while carbon has 2p orbitals, leading to a larger energy gap in silicon's atomic orbitals. Phosphorous (78) and sulfur (79), both with 3p orbitals and more electronegative than silicon, exhibit expected redshifted emissions.[74,75] In the case of ketone addition in this position (80), it is highly electron-withdrawing, therefore, resulting in emission at 911\mathrm{nm} .[69] Therefore, when predicting HOMO-LUMO gap for heteroatom substitution within the conjugated core, one must consider electronegativity and compare the heteroatom's FMOs (3p vs 2p).

page_payload.translation_unit_protected_source_text: Consider ethene and butadiene LCAO, shown in Figure 8a. When a molecule has a shorter \pi -conjugated system, there are fewer p-orbitals involved in the overlapping, and the energy difference between the bonding and anti-bonding orbitals (HOMO-LUMO gap) is relatively larger. On the other hand, in a molecule with an extended \pi -conjugated system, more p-orbitals are involved in the overlapping. As a result, the energy difference between the bonding and anti-bonding orbitals becomes smaller, causing a decrease in the HOMO-LUMO gap (butadiene, Figure 8a).

page_payload.source_text: Red-shifting cyanine emission through conjugation extension is well established. Either the methylene bridge is extended, or aryl fusions are added to the phenyl ring. Cy3 (61) has an emission of 575\mathrm{nm} . Extension of the bridge to Cy5 (62) shifts the fluorescence to 677\mathrm{nm} .[58] Further bridge extension to Cy7 (63) affords emission at 789\mathrm{nm} .[61] Unfortunately, bridge extension and red-shifting cyanine fluorescence comes at the cost of diminished quantum yields. Additional benzofusions to Cy3 and Cy5 shifts the fluorescence 27\mathrm{nm} to the red (64 vs 61 and 65 vs 62).[61]

page_payload.translation_unit_protected_translated_te: 通过共轭延伸实现花青染料发射红移是成熟的方法。通常通过延长亚甲基桥或在苯环上引入芳环稠合来实现。Cy3 (61) 的发射波长为 575 nm。将桥延长至 Cy5 (62) 可将荧光移至 677 nm。[58] 进一步将桥延长至 Cy7 (63) 可获得 789 nm 的发射。[61] 然而，桥的延长和花青荧光的红移是以量子产率降低为代价的。在 Cy3 和 Cy5 上额外进行苯并稠合可使荧光红移 27 nm（64 对比 61，以及 65 对比 62）。[61]

page_payload.source_text: Aryl ethenylene addition on the BODIPY scaffold can dramatically shift the spectral properties. The more aryl ethenylenes incorporated, the further red the fluorescence.[62] For instance, one aryl ethenylene extends emission to 587~\mathrm{nm} (66), while four of them push it even further to 709~\mathrm{nm} (67).[62] In contrast, without this \pi -extension, the emission occurs at 519~\mathrm{nm} (34).[36] Combining EDG and extending BODIPY conjugation can shift the emission to the near-infrared ( 780~\mathrm{nm} ).[66] Furthermore, conjugation can be extended off the pyrroles (68 or 69).[63] However, addition of aryl or ethenylene substituents at the meso-position does not result in significant red-shifting. The \pi -system in the meso-position is perpendicular to the \pi -system of the BODIPY core, therefore, is not in conjugation with the rest of the molecule (62 versus 63).[63]

page_payload.translation_unit_protected_translated_te: 在BODIPY骨架上引入芳基乙烯基可显著改变其光谱性质。引入的芳基乙烯基越多，荧光发射波长红移越显著。[62] 例如，引入一个芳基乙烯基可将发射波长延伸至587 nm（66），而引入四个则可进一步推至709 nm（67）。[62] 相比之下，若无此$\pi$扩展，发射波长位于519 nm（34）。[36] 结合给电子基团（EDG）并扩展BODIPY共轭体系，可将发射波长移至近红外区（780 nm）。[66] 此外，共轭体系可从吡咯环向外延伸（68或69）。[63] 然而，在中位引入芳基或乙烯基取代基并不会导致显著的红移。中位$\pi$体系与BODIPY核心的$\pi$体系垂直，因此不与分子的其余部分共轭（62与63对比）。[63]

page_payload.source_text: Replacing oxygen with a less electronegative nitrogen (74) leads to an expected blue-shift in the emission wavelength, from 562\mathrm{nm} to 528\mathrm{nm} .[70] However, substituting with a less electronegative carbon atom (76) surprisingly causes a red-shift in the emission. This is because nitrogen and oxygen have lone pairs that can donate into the pi-system, reducing their ability to stabilize the LUMO compared to carbon, despite being more electronegative. Interestingly, substituting carbon with silicon results in an even further red-shift for silicon-rhodamine 77, with an emission at 648\mathrm{nm} , despite silicon's lower electronegativity.[71] This is because the energy levels of atom's p-orbitals (Figure 9b) must be considered, since the FMOs are derived from the linear combination of atomic orbitals (LCAOs).[79] Silicon possesses 3p orbitals, while carbon has 2p orbitals, leading to a larger energy gap in silicon's atomic orbitals. Phosphorous (78) and sulfur (79), both with 3p orbitals and more electronegative than silicon, exhibit expected redshifted emissions.[74,75] In the case of ketone addition in this position (80), it is highly electron-withdrawing, therefore, resulting in emission at 911\mathrm{nm} .[69] Therefore, when predicting HOMO-LUMO gap for heteroatom substitution within the conjugated core, one must consider electronegativity and compare the heteroatom's FMOs (3p vs 2p).

saved_item.source_text: Replacing oxygen with a less electronegative nitrogen (74) leads to an expected blue-shift in the emission wavelength, from 562\mathrm{nm} to 528\mathrm{nm} .[70] However, substituting with a less electronegative carbon atom (76) surprisingly causes a red-shift in the emission. This is because nitrogen and oxygen have lone pairs that can donate into the pi-system, reducing their ability to stabilize the LUMO compared to carbon, despite being more electronegative. Interestingly, substituting carbon with silicon results in an even further red-shift for silicon-rhodamine 77, with an emission at 648\mathrm{nm} , despite silicon's lower electronegativity.[71] This is because the energy levels of atom's p-orbitals (Figure 9b) must be considered, since the FMOs are derived from the linear combination of atomic orbitals (LCAOs).[79] Silicon possesses 3p orbitals, while carbon has 2p orbitals, leading to a larger energy gap in silicon's atomic orbitals. Phosphorous (78) and sulfur (79), both with 3p orbitals and more electronegative than silicon, exhibit expected redshifted emissions.[74,75] In the case of ketone addition in this position (80), it is highly electron-withdrawing, therefore, resulting in emission at 911\mathrm{nm} .[69] Therefore, when predicting HOMO-LUMO gap for heteroatom substitution within the conjugated core, one must consider electronegativity and compare the heteroatom's FMOs (3p vs 2p).

replay_result.translated_text: 用电负性较低的氮原子（74）取代氧原子，预计会导致发射波长蓝移，从 $562\mathrm{nm}$ 变为 $528\mathrm{nm}$。[70] 然而，用电负性更低的碳原子（76）取代却出人意料地导致了发射红移。这是因为氮和氧具有孤对电子，可以贡献到 $\pi$ 体系中，与碳相比，尽管它们电负性更强，但其稳定 LUMO 的能力反而降低了。 有趣的是，尽管硅的电负性较低，但用硅取代碳会导致硅罗丹明77发生更显著的红移，其发射波长位于648 nm。[71] 这是因为必须考虑原子p轨道的能级（图9b），因为前线分子轨道源自原子轨道的线性组合（LCAO）。[79] 硅具有3p轨道，而碳具有2p轨道，这导致硅原子轨道的能隙更大。 磷（78）和硫（79）均具有 3p 轨道且电负性高于硅，表现出预期的红移发射。[74,75] 在此位置引入酮基（80）时，由于其强吸电子性，导致发射位于 911 nm。[69] 因此，在预测共轭核心内杂原子取代的 HOMO-LUMO 能隙时，必须考虑电负性并比较杂原子的前线分子轨道（3p 与 2p）。
